name: Azure Blob Storage REST API
what_it_does: Azure Blob Storage REST API provides scalable object storage for unstructured data — store, retrieve, and manage blobs (files) in containers with tiered storage, versioning, lifecycle management, and CDN integration.
use_cases: Storing agent-generated files (reports, documents, exports) in Azure for durable access
Generating SAS (Shared Access Signature) tokens for temporary, scoped access to blobs
ML data lakes for training datasets, model artifacts, and inference outputs on Azure
Triggering event-driven agent workflows on blob creation via Azure Event Grid
CDN origin for static assets with Azure Front Door or CDN profile
not_for: Structured/relational data queries (use Azure SQL, Cosmos DB, or Synapse)
Teams on AWS or GCP — S3 or GCS are better aligned with existing infrastructure
Very high-frequency small object operations (Azure Queue or Table are more efficient)
best_when: Your agent infrastructure is on Azure and you need reliable, scalable object storage integrated with Azure IAM, Event Grid, and the Microsoft ecosystem.
avoid_when: You're primarily on AWS or GCP — cross-cloud egress costs and SDK inconsistency make Azure Blob a poor choice outside the Azure ecosystem.

auth.notes: Three auth methods: Azure AD (OAuth2 + RBAC — recommended), Shared Key (account key, all-or-nothing), SAS token (time-limited, scoped URL). Managed Identity for Azure-hosted agents eliminates credential management. SAS tokens for external/temporary access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 5 GB LRS storage free for 12 months; first year free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Hot tier: $0.018/GB/month; Cool: $0.01/GB/month; Archive: $0.00099/GB/month. Operations and egress additional.
pricing.notes: Costs: storage + operations + egress. Egress within Azure region is free. Globally redundant storage (GRS) is ~2x the price of LRS.

performance.rate_limits: Up to 20,000 requests/second per storage account (scalable)

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Azure standard XML error responses with Code and Message. SDK translates to typed exceptions. HTTP 412 Precondition Failed is common for conditional operations — important for concurrency patterns.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: PUT operations are idempotent (overwrite by default). Conditional puts via If-None-Match: * prevent overwrites. Block blobs support staged upload with idempotent block PutBlock calls.
known_agent_gotchas: Storage account keys are all-or-nothing — use SAS tokens or Managed Identity for least-privilege agent access
SAS tokens have no revocation mechanism — once generated, valid until expiry; plan expiry carefully
Blob tiers (Hot/Cool/Archive) have different access latencies — Archive rehydration takes hours; agents must not expect instant access
Public blob access is disabled by default at account level — agents cannot make blobs public without explicit account-level change
SDK and REST API have subtle behavioral differences — test thoroughly if mixing raw HTTP with Azure SDK in same codebase

security_score_components.security_notes: Azure RBAC with fine-grained blob/container permissions. Managed Identity eliminates credentials for Azure-hosted agents. Customer-managed keys (BYOK) supported. Private endpoints for network isolation. FedRAMP High, HIPAA, ISO 27001.
